recipeOptions.name: Plain Rice Idli
instructions: Wash and soak rice and dal separately for 4-6 hours.
Grind dal to a smooth fluffy batter. Grind rice to a slightly coarse batter.
Mix both, add salt, and ferment overnight (8-10 hours).
Pour batter into greased idli plates.
Steam for 10-12 minutes on medium heat. Serve hot with chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Rava Idli
instructions: Roast rava on low heat for 5 mins until aromatic. Let it cool.
Temper mustard seeds, cashews, and grated carrots. Add to the rava.
Mix rava, tempering, and curd. Rest for 15 mins.
Add water to reach idli batter consistency. Stir in Eno fruit salt.
Pour into idli steamer and steam for 10-12 mins.

recipeOptions.name: Foxtail Millet Idli
instructions: Soak millet and urad dal separately for 5-6 hours.
Grind them to a smooth batter. Mix and let ferment overnight.
Grease idli molds and pour the fermented batter.
Steam for 10-15 minutes.
Serve soft and healthy millet idlis with sambar.

recipeOptions.name: Masala Dosa
instructions: Boil and mash the potatoes. Thinly slice onions.
Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, sautè onions, add turmeric and potatoes to make the masala.
Spread dosa batter thin and wide on a hot tawa.
Drizzle ghee along the edges. Place the potato masala in the center.
Fold and serve crispy.

recipeOptions.name: Set Dosa
instructions: To make set dosa batter, blend soaked poha along with rice/dal before fermentation.
Pour a ladle of batter onto a hot tawa. Do NOT spread it thin; let it be thick and spongy.
Drizzle oil/ghee around the edges, cover with a lid, and cook for a minute.
Serve in a set of 2-3 with saagu or vegetable kurma.

recipeOptions.name: Ragi Dosa
instructions: Mix ragi flour, rice flour, onions, cumin, and salt.
Add plenty of water to make a thin, watery batter.
Pour the batter starting from the edges of a hot tawa towards the center (like rava dosa).
Cook until crisp on low-medium heat. Flip if needed.
Serve hot with coconut chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Kanda Poha
instructions: Rinse poha in a colander until soft. Drain water completely.
Heat oil, roast peanuts and set aside.
Temper mustard seeds, sautè onions until translucent. Add turmeric and salt.
Gently mix in the softened poha. Cover and steam on low heat for 3 mins.
Garnish with peanuts, fresh coriander, and a squeeze of lemon.

recipeOptions.name: Indori Poha
instructions: Rinse poha. Add a pinch of sugar, salt, and turmeric directly to the poha and mix.
Temper mustard seeds, fennel seeds, and green chilies in hot oil.
Add poha, splash a little water, and STEAM cook covered for a soft texture.
Plate the poha, aggressively sprinkle Jeeravan Masala, onions, and Ratlami sev.
Serve immediately.

recipeOptions.name: Aloo Poha
instructions: Rinse the poha and let it drain.
Heat oil, temper mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add diced potatoes.
Cover and cook the potatoes until soft and lightly browned.
Add turmeric, salt, and the drained poha. Mix gently.
Cook for 2 minutes and garnish with fresh coriander.

recipeOptions.name: Rava Upma
instructions: Dry roast rava until aromatic and set aside.
Heat ghee/oil, splutter mustard seeds, cumin, urad dal, and chana dal.
Sautè chopped onions, green chilies, and ginger.
Add 350ml boiling water and salt to the pan.
Slowly pour roasted rava while stirring continuously to prevent lumps. Cover and cook for 2 mins.

recipeOptions.name: Oats Upma
instructions: Dry roast the oats slightly to prevent stickiness. Keep aside.
Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, and sauté veggies until tender.
Add water (ratio 1:1.5 of oats to water) and salt. Let it boil.
Stir in the oats, cover, and cook on low heat for 3-4 mins until water absorbs.
Switch off heat, add lemon juice and fluff.

recipeOptions.name: Vermicelli (Semiya) Upma
instructions: Roast vermicelli in a pan until golden (if not pre-roasted).
Temper mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves in oil. Sautè onions.
Add water (approx 1.5 cups per cup of vermicelli) and let it come to a boil. Add salt.
Add roasted vermicelli. Turn heat to low, cover and cook until moisture is absorbed.
Fluff up and serve hot.

recipeOptions.name: Aloo Paratha
instructions: Knead the wheat flour with water and a pinch of salt into a soft dough. Rest 15 min.
Boil, peel, and mash potatoes. Mix with garam masala, ajwain, chopped chilies, coriander, and salt.
Pinch a ball of dough, flatten it, and stuff a portion of potato filling inside. Seal the edges.
Roll gently into a thick circle.
Cook on a hot tawa with generous ghee/butter until golden brown spots appear on both sides.

recipeOptions.name: Paneer Paratha
instructions: Prepare a soft wheat dough as usual.
Mix grated paneer with cumin powder, chopped coriander, green chilies, and salt for the stuffing.
Stuff the dough balls with the paneer mix and roll out carefully.
Roast on a hot tawa with oil/ghee.
Serve hot with curd and pickle.

recipeOptions.name: Gobi Paratha
instructions: Prepare soft dough.
Grate cauliflower finely. Squeeze out excess water if any. Mix with amchur, turmeric, chopped green chilies, and salt.
Fill dough with the gobi mixture and roll out.
Cook on a tawa using ghee until crispy on both sides.
Note: Add salt to gobi just before stuffing so it doesn't release water.

recipeOptions.name: Besan Chilla
instructions: Mix besan, finely chopped onions, tomatoes, ajwain, turmeric, and salt.
Gradually add water to make a smooth, pourable batter free of lumps.
Heat a tawa, pour a ladle of batter, and spread it in a circular motion.
Cook with a few drops of oil around the edges until golden on one side. Flip and cook briefly.
Serve with green chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Moong Dal Chilla
instructions: Soak moong dal for 2-3 hours.
Blend dal with ginger, green chili, and cumin seeds into a smooth batter. Add salt.
Spread batter on a hot greased tawa like a dosa.
Cook on medium heat. Optional: grate paneer over the top while cooking.
Fold and serve hot.

recipeOptions.name: Oats Chilla
instructions: Grind oats into a fine powder. Mix with besan.
Add yogurt, water, grated carrots, salt, and spices to form a batter. Rest 10 minutes.
Pour and spread onto a hot non-stick pan.
Cook evenly on both sides until cooked through.
Serve with ketchup or chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Appam with Veg Stew
instructions: Pour a ladle of appam batter into an appachatti. Swirl it to coat the sides thin, leaving a thick center.
Cover and cook for 2 mins on medium heat until edges are crisp and center is soft.
For Stew: Boil veggies with whole spices, ginger, and green chilies in thin coconut milk.
Once veggies are cooked, add thick coconut milk. Gentle simmer (do not boil hard).
Serve appams with the warm, fragrant stew.

recipeOptions.name: Appam with Chicken Stew
instructions: Make appams similar to the veg stew recipe.
For Chicken Stew: Sauté chicken, onions, and curry leaves with whole spices.
Add water/thin coconut milk and cook chicken till tender.
Finish with thick coconut milk, a pinch of pepper, and fry cashews & raisins for garnish.
Dunk appams into the rich gravy.

recipeOptions.name: Appam with Egg Roast
instructions: Make fluffy lacy appams.
Boil, peel, and slit the eggs.
Heat oil, deeply caramelize the sliced onions. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes.
Add spice powders, cook until oil separates. Toss the boiled eggs into the masala.
Serve spicy egg roast with the mildly sweet appams.

recipeOptions.name: Ven Pongal
instructions: Dry roast moong dal lightly, wash with rice.
Pressure cook rice and dal with water (1:4 ratio) and salt for 4 whistles until mushy.
In a small pan, heat ghee, slightly crush peppercorns & cumin, and temper with curry leaves and cashews.
Pour tempering over the mashed rice/dal, mix well.
Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.

recipeOptions.name: Sweet/Sakkarai Pongal
instructions: Cook rice and lightly roasted dal till extremely soft.
Melt jaggery with a little water, strain it for impurities, and add to the cooked rice-dal mix.
Cook together for 5 mins so the flavors merge.
Fry cashews and raisins in generous ghee, add to pongal along with cardamom.
Mix well and serve as a sweet treat.

recipeOptions.name: Millet Pongal
instructions: Replace rice with washed millets. Lightly roast dal.
Pressure cook the millet and dal with salt and water until softly mashed.
Heat ghee, do the standard cumin, pepper, cashew, and curry leaf tempering.
Mix tempering into the mushy millets.
A highly nutritious alternative to regular pongal.

recipeOptions.name: Rice Puttu with Kadala
instructions: For Puttu: Sprinkle water on rice flour and salt, rub until it's crumbly but holds shape when pressed. Rest 15 min.
Layer puttu maker with grated coconut and the damp flour. Steam for 5-7 mins until steam escapes top.
For Kadala Curry: Pressure cook soaked chickpeas.
Roast grated coconut with spices, blend to a paste, cook with chickpeas and onions until gravy thickens.
Serve the crumbly puttu mashed with the spicy kadala curry.

recipeOptions.name: Ragi Puttu
instructions: Sprinkle warm water & salt over ragi flour to get a damp, crumbly texture without lumps.
Fill the puttu cylinder with alternating layers of coconut and ragi flour.
Steam for 5-7 minutes.
Serve with ripe bananas and a sprinkle of sugar, mashed together with hands.

recipeOptions.name: Wheat Puttu
instructions: Dampen wheat flour with salt water to a crumbly consistency. Steam with alternating coconut layers.
Pressure cook whole green gram with salt and a pinch of turmeric.
Top the steamed wheat puttu with green gram, a bit of extra grated coconut if desired.
Serve with crunchy pappadams.

recipeOptions.name: Bombay Veg Sandwich
instructions: Butter the bread slices and spread a generous layer of green coriander-mint chutney.
Thinly slice the boiled potatoes, cucumbers, tomatoes, and onions.
Layer the veggies inside the bread, heavily sprinkling chaat masala between layers.
Close the sandwich, cut edges if preferred, and serve fresh. (Optional: grill it).

recipeOptions.name: Paneer Tikka Sandwich
instructions: Sautè paneer cubes, chopped capsicum, and onions with tikka masala and a little oil until cooked.
Place the mixture on bread slices, optionally place a cheese slice.
Grill/toast in a sandwich maker or tawa until bread is golden and crisp.
Serve hot with ketchup.

recipeOptions.name: Curd/Yogurt Sandwich
instructions: Mix hung thick curd with finely chopped veggies, salt, and black pepper to form a creamy spread.
Spread the mixture evenly on bread slices.
Toast the sandwich on a hot tawa with a little butter until crisp.
The inside will remain creamy while the outside is crispy.

recipeOptions.name: Punjabi Rajma Masala
instructions: Soak Rajma overnight. Pressure cook until very soft (about 6-8 whistles).
Sautè finely chopped onions until deep golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato purèe.
Add coriander powder, chili powder, and turmeric. Cook until oil separates.
Add the soft rajma along with its water. Simmer for 15 minutes. Finish with garam masala and coriander.
Cook basmati rice fluffy. Serve hot rajma over rice.

recipeOptions.name: Jammu Rajma (No Onion/Garlic)
instructions: Soak rajma and pressure cook until tender.
Heat mustard oil to smoking point, lower heat, add cumin, heeng, and dry red chilies.
Pour in tomato purèe and whisked yogurt. Stir vigorously to prevent curdling.
Add cooked rajma and simmer gently. The sauce will be rich and tangy.
Serve with plain boiled rice.

recipeOptions.name: Low-oil Rajma Pulao
instructions: In a pressure cooker, heat a little ghee, temper whole spices and saute onions until golden.
Add a little ginger-garlic paste, then toss in the pre-boiled rajma, mint leaves, and soaked rice.
Add water (1.5 cups per cup of rice) and salt. Mix gently.
Pressure cook for 2 whistles on medium heat. Fluff and serve with raita.

recipeOptions.name: Madras Sambar
instructions: Pressure cook toor dal with a pinch of turmeric until completely mashed.
Boil chopped vegetables in a pot with tamarind extract, sambar powder, and salt. Cook until tender.
Add the mashed dal to the boiling vegetables. Adjust water for consistency.
Temper mustard seeds, fenugreek, dry red chilies, and curry leaves in hot sesame oil.
Pour tempering into sambar, garnish with coriander. Serve with steamed rice.

recipeOptions.name: Udupi Sambar
instructions: Dry roast coriander seeds, chana dal, urad dal, and byadagi chilies. Blend to a paste with fresh grated coconut.
Cook toor dal till slightly mushy. Boil vegetables in tamarind water and salt.
Once veggies are cooked, add the fresh coconut spice paste and jaggery. Bring to a rapid boil.
Mix in the mashed dal to thicken. Temper with mustard and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Serve the slightly sweet and spicy sambar with hot rice.

recipeOptions.name: Kerala Sambar (Varutharacha)
instructions: Roast grated coconut with shallots and curry leaves till deep brown. Add dry spice powders and blend into a very smooth, dark paste.
Boil mixed vegetables with tamarind and salt. Stir in softly cooked toor dal.
Add the roasted coconut paste (Varutharacha) to the pot. Stir well and simmer until thick.
Temper mustard seeds and dry chilies in coconut oil. Pour over sambar.
Serve with Kerala Matta rice.

recipeOptions.name: Dhaba Style Dal Makhani
instructions: Soak urad dal and rajma overnight. Pressure cook them patiently (approx 8-10 whistles) with salt until butter-soft.
In a heavy-bottomed pan, add butter and sautè ginger-garlic paste loudly. Whisk in tomato purèe and chili powder.
Cook the masala down until fat rises. Add the mashed dal with its murky water.
Simmer aggressively on very low heat for at least an hour, stirring frequently and adding water as needed.
Finish with heavy cream, kasoori methi, a large dollop of butter, and serve with jeera rice.

recipeOptions.name: Quick Restaurant Dal Makhani
instructions: Pressure cook soaked dal with salt and a pinch of baking soda to speed up cooking (just 4-5 whistles needed).
Sautè the onion tomato paste in butter, add spice powders, and cook until thick.
Toss in cooked dal, mash roughly with the back of a ladle. Simmer for 15-20 mins.
Stir in cream just before serving.
Serve over hot jeera rice.

recipeOptions.name: Vegan/Homestyle Black Dal
instructions: Boil the soaked dal gently until done, without over-mashing.
Heat oil in a pan, add cumin and heavily brown the chopped garlic. Then sautè chopped onions.
Add a pinch of turmeric and chili powder.
Pour the cooked dal into the tempering. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Serve simple and comforting dal over plain rice, skipping the heavy cream completely.

recipeOptions.name: Creamy Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach leaves in boiling water for 2 mins, immediately plunge into ice water to retain green color.
Blend spinach with a green chili into a smooth purèe.
Heat oil, sautè crushed garlic and chopped onions until translucent. Stir in coriander powder and a pinch of turmeric.
Pour the spinach purèe, add salt and simmer gently for 5 mins.
Add paneer cubes and a dash of fresh cream. Let paneer warm through without boiling heavily.
Serve with soft chapatis.

recipeOptions.name: Dhaba Palak Paneer
instructions: Finely chop a portion of the raw spinach and boil the rest; roughly blend the boiled spinach.
Lightly pan-fry the paneer cubes until slightly golden and keep them soaked in warm water.
Heat ghee, dry red chilies, cumin. Add onions and tomatoes to make a coarse, rustic masala base.
Mix in chopped spinach, cook until it wilts, then pour the blended spinach.
Fold in the soft paneer cubes and let simmer tightly covered. Flavor with strong garam masala.
Serve robustly on rice or naan.

recipeOptions.name: Vegan Palak Tofu
instructions: Press the tofu block to remove excess water. Cut into cubes. Optionally air-fry the tofu cubes for better texture.
Blanch and purèe the spinach as usual.
In oil, fry the onion and garlic paste until golden, avoid butter/ghee entirely.
Combine spinach purèe, add cashew paste for a creamy thick texture, salt and lemon to cut the bitterness.
Toss in tofu and let the gravy heat the cubes through. Enjoy with rice.

recipeOptions.name: Traditional Kerala Avial

ingredients.name: Mixed Veg (Plantain, Yam, Carrot, Beans, Drumstick, Ash Gourd)

instructions: Cut all vegetables uniformly into 2-inch batons.
Cook them lightly in a pot with a pinch of turmeric, salt, and half a cup of water until barely tender but firm.
Coarsely crush grated coconut with green chilies, cumin, and small shallot without adding water.
Mix the crushed coconut over the cooked vegetables.
Stir in sour curd (or raw mango earlier) and quickly turn off heat.
Drizzle raw coconut oil and curry leaves on top, keep covered till served with matta rice.

recipeOptions.name: Tamil-Style Avial
instructions: Dice vegetables in cubes or short batons. Boil with turmeric and salt.
Instead of curd, use a very mild tamarind extract for sourness.
Grind coconut, green chilies, and cumin into a slightly smoother paste. Stir in a tiny bit of rice flour to bind.
Add to vegetables, boil gently for 2 minutes to thicken.
Finish with curry leaves and coconut oil. This pairs perfectly with Adai (lentil crepe) or steamed rice.

recipeOptions.name: Mixed Veg Morkootu
instructions: Cook the ash gourd and carrots with turmeric and salt.
Grind coconut and cumin seeds entirely smooth, unlike rough crushed avial mix.
Stir cooked toor dal to thicken, whisked curd, and the coconut paste with the boiled veggies.
Bring just to a gentle simmer (do not boil, it will split).
Temper mustard seeds in oil and pour over morkootu. Serve gently over white rice.

recipeOptions.name: Hyderabadi Chicken Dum Biryani
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, biryani masala, ginger-garlic paste, half of the fried onions, mint, coriander, and oil for 2+ hours.
Par-boil basmati rice with whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, bay leaf) until 70% cooked. Drain water completely.
Layer the raw marinated chicken at the bottom of a heavy pot. Pile the half-cooked rice on top.
Sprinkle saffron milk, remaining fried onions, and ghee on the top rice layer.
Seal the pot with dough/foil (Dum) and cook on low heat for 35-40 mins.
Rest for 10 minutes, open, and gently mix from the bottom. Serve hot with raita.

recipeOptions.name: Thalassery Chicken Biryani
instructions: Slowly fry plenty of onions until deeply caramelized to make the masala base. Add tomatoes to cook thoroughly into a thick paste (no raw masala taste).
Cook chicken gently in this thick, semi-dry onion-tomato gravy with Malabar spices.
Wash short-grain Kaima rice. Gently fry rice in ghee, then cook it till done.
Layer cooked rice and cooked chicken masala alternately in a pan.
Garnish generously with fried onions, ghee roasted cashews, and sultana raisins. Dum for 10-15 mins.

recipeOptions.name: Veg Dum Biryani (Vegetarian)
instructions: Steam/blanch vegetables lightly. Marinate them and paneer cubes in thick yogurt and biryani spices.
Cook rice separately with whole spices until 80% done. Drain well.
Lightly cook the veg marinade in a heavy pot until fat shows at the edges.
Layer the cooked vegetables and par-cooked rice. Apply saffron milk.
Cover and Dum cook for 20 mins. The delicate veggies will not overcook if done properly.

recipeOptions.name: Amritsari Chole with Bhature
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight. Pressure cook them along with tea bags and a pinch of baking soda till perfectly dark and soft.
Sautè onion paste till brown, add tomato purèe and Chole Masala. Mix cooked chickpeas with splash of cooking water.
Simmer chole till gravy clings to beans.
For Bhatura: Knead maida with yogurt, soda, salt, and water. Rest for 2 hours.
Roll out into ovals and deep fry in hot oil until fully puffed up.
Serve spicy chole with hot fluffy bhaturas.

recipeOptions.name: Pindi Chole (Dry/Semi-dry)
instructions: Boil chickpeas till soft (with tea for color). Discard excess water entirely.
In a large iron kadhai, add chickpeas. Sprinkle all dry spice powders heavily including Anardana over them.
Heat a large chunk of ghee in a ladle, fry chopped ginger, green chilies, and whole spices until sizzling.
Pour the scorching hot ghee directly over the dry spices on the chickpeas. Mix thoroughly.
Cook together for 5 mins for a robust, dry dark chole. Serve with puri or naan.

recipeOptions.name: Street Style Chole Rice (Chawal)
instructions: Cook chickpeas until soft.
Heat oil, lightly scramble the onion-tomato paste, adding chana masala early to steep the flavors.
Mix boiled chickpeas along with large amounts of cooking liquid. Boil aggressively so the grave thickens into a soupy consistency.
Cook basic plain white rice.
Pour soupy, spicy chole over a massive plate of rice. Serve with raw onion rings and green chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Punjabi Kadhi Pakora
instructions: Whisk very sour yogurt and roughly 30g of besan with 4 cups water. Ensure no lumps.
Temper mustard, fenugreek, and cumin in oil, pour in yogurt mixture. Bring to a rapid boil, stirring constantly, then simmer for 40 mins.
Make a thick batter with remainder besan, sliced onions/potatoes, spices, and a speck of baking soda.
Deep fry small irregular clumps of batter to make crispy pakoras.
Drop pakoras into boiling kadhi right before serving. Serve over steaming white rice.

recipeOptions.name: Gujarati Kadhi (Sweet & Sour)
instructions: Mix yogurt, very little besan (for a thin consistency), water, jaggery, and salt.
In a pan, heat ghee, temper with mustard, cumin, dried red chili, curry leaves, cinnamon, and cloves.
Pour in yogurt mixture slowly while stirring continuously to avoid splitting.
Gently boil it for 10-15 minutes to take off the raw besan edge.
Serve distinctly sweet, thin, white kadhi alongside khichdi or hot rice.

recipeOptions.name: Sindhi Kadhi

ingredients.name: Mixed Veg (Okra, Drumstick, Cluster Beans, Potato)

instructions: Unlike Punjabi kadhi, do not use yogurt. Instead, roast besan slowly in oil until dark reddish-brown and nutty.
Add plenty of water rapidly to the roasted besan to create a brown soup. Keep it lump-free.
Add vegetables simultaneously (lightly fry okra beforehand) along with dry spices.
Boil for 30 minutes until vegetables are cooked and the soup thickens nicely.
Add tamarind pulp at the end for pure tanginess. Serve hot with rice.

recipeOptions.name: Moru Curry (Tempered Buttermilk)
instructions: Chop ginger, green chili, and shallots finely.
Whisk yogurt/buttermilk with a pinch of turmeric and salt. Keep it smooth.
Heat coconut oil, splutter mustard, fenugreek, dry red chili, and curry leaves.
Sautè ginger, green chilies, and shallots momentarily until aromatic.
Lower the heat completely. Pour tempered oil mix over whisked yogurt (do NOT boil). Serve with rice.

recipeOptions.name: Manga Pulissery (Ripe Mango Curry)
instructions: Peel and cook ripe mango chunks with turmeric and very little water until soft.
Grind fresh coconut, cumin, and green chili to a super fine paste.
Add coconut paste to the cooked mangoes and cook down firmly until thick.
Lower heat completely and gently mix in beaten sour yogurt. Heat until it starts to steam but don't boil.
Temper mustard seeds in coconut oil and pour over top.

recipeOptions.name: Kurukku Kalan
instructions: Chop yam/raw banana into small chunks. Cook them thoroughly with lots of black pepper and turmeric.
Pour slightly sour yogurt into the pot and vigorously boil it down continuously. This needs to dramatically reduce (Kurukku).
While reducing, blend coconut and cumin into a dense paste.
Once the yogurt mixture is thick, dark yellow and coating the vegetables, add the coconut paste and gently simmer.
Finish with coconut oil tempering. Serve alongside a sadhya feast.

recipeOptions.name: Kerala Meen Curry (Coconut Tamarind)
instructions: Soak kudampuli in warm water. Clean and cut fish.
Sautè sliced shallots, ginger, garlic, and curry leaves heavily in coconut oil in a clay meen chatti.
Lower flame, add loads of red chili powder, coriander, and turmeric powder. Roast until oil separates.
Pour in kudampuli with its water. Add salt and let it boil into a red roaring gravy.
Drop the fish pieces, swirl the pot, cover, and gently cook for 10-15 mins. Serve hot over matta rice.

recipeOptions.name: Goan Fish Curry
instructions: Blend fresh coconut, pre-soaked Kashmiri chilies, coriander seeds, cumin, garlic, and tamarind into an incredibly smooth, fine, deep-red paste.
Pour this paste into a pan. Adjust the gravy thickness with minor water addition.
Bring the gravy to a simmer, add a diced onion and slit green chilies if desired.
Toss the cleaned seafood into the gravy. Let it poach gently for 5-8 minutes.
Serve the slightly sweet/tangy bright red coconut curry with steamed white rice.

recipeOptions.name: Mangalorean Fish Curry (Pulimunchi)
instructions: Dry roast byadagi chilies and dry spices individually until deeply fragrant.
Blend the roasted spices, garlic, onion, and tamarind to a thick, dark spice paste without coconut.
Heat a pan, lightly toast a small amount of onion, then pour the dark spice paste and boil fiercely.
Slide the fish pieces. Pulimunchi translates to "sour and chili". Adjust tamarind/chili to bite the palate. Cook for 8-10 mins.
Serve famously with boiled red rice or neer dosa.

recipeOptions.name: Creamy Tomato Soup
instructions: In a pan, melt butter. Sauté chopped garlic, onion, and a bay leaf.
Add roughly chopped tomatoes, a pinch of salt and sugar, and whole peppercorns.
Cook until tomatoes break down entirely. Add water and simmer for 10 minutes.
Discard bay leaf, blend smooth, and strain the soup for a silky texture.
Warm the soup, add a splash of cream and fresh pepper. Serve with crisp garlic toast.

recipeOptions.name: Sweet Corn Veg Soup
instructions: Blend half the sweet corn into a smooth paste.
Boil 3 cups of water/stock in a pot. Add the corn paste, whole corn kernels, and finely diced veggies.
Simmer until veggies are tender but crunchy (about 5-7 mins).
Stir in the corn starch slurry continuously until the soup thickens nicely.
Season with pepper, salt, and maybe a dash of soy sauce. Serve piping hot.

recipeOptions.name: Mushroom Clear Soup
instructions: In a pot, gently sauté crushed garlic, celery stems, and sliced mushrooms until the mushrooms shrink.
Pour in 3-4 cups of water/vegetable stock. Let it simmer gently on low heat for 15-20 minutes.
Season heavily with freshly ground black pepper and salt.
Turn off heat, stir in a dash of lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves.
A very light, comforting dinner paired well with toasted brown bread.

recipeOptions.name: Moong Dal Khichdi
instructions: Wash rice and dal together. You can lightly dry roast the dal for better flavor.
Add them to a pressure cooker with 3.5 to 4 times the water, a pinch of turmeric, and salt.
Pressure cook for 3-4 whistles until very soft and mushy.
In a small pan, heat ghee, splutter cumin seeds, heeng, and optionally a dry red chili.
Pour the tempering over the hot mushy khichdi. Mix and serve immediately.

recipeOptions.name: Masala Veg Khichdi
instructions: Heat ghee in a pressure cooker. Temper cumin, cloves, and bay leaf.
Saute sliced onions, ginger-garlic paste, and tomatoes until soft.
Add turmeric, red chili powder, chopped vegetables, and the washed rice-dal mix.
Add water (approx 3.5 cups), salt, and pressure cook until mushy.
Finish with a sprinkle of garam masala and fresh coriander. Great for slightly heavier winter dinners.

recipeOptions.name: Bajra Khichdi (Millet)
instructions: Lightly pulse/crush the Bajra in a blender to remove the husk. Winnow or wash to remove floating husk bits.
Soak the bajra for hours, then pressure cook it alone first for 4 whistles.
Add the washed moong dal to the cooked bajra, add water and salt, and pressure cook again for 2 more whistles.
Heat lavish ghee in a pan, toss in crushed pepper and cumin seeds. Pour over the starchy, thick khichdi.
Extremely warming and nutritious.

recipeOptions.name: Temple Style Curd Rice
instructions: Cook rice with extra water until very mushy. Mash it further while it is hot.
Let the rice cool completely. Stir in milk (prevents excessive souring) and large amounts of fresh curd.
In a pan, heat oil, temper mustard seeds, urad dal, finely chopped ginger, green chilies, and curry leaves.
Pour the tempering over the curd rice. Add salt and mix thoroughly.
Serve with pickle or roasted papad. Eaten chilled or room temperature.

recipeOptions.name: Pomegranate & Grape Curd Rice
instructions: Take cooled, over-cooked, mashed rice. Whip in the fresh curd till remarkably creamy.
Gently fold in fresh ruby red pomegranate arils and chopped sweet grapes.
Fry broken cashews in oil, along with mustard seeds and curry leaves.
Mix the tempering into the yogurt rice.
A delightfully sweet, savory, and cooling light dinner.

recipeOptions.name: Tadka Curd Rice (Bagala Bath)
instructions: Mix cool rice and yogurt aggressively with salt.
Toss in raw grated carrots, cucumber pieces, and vibrant minced coriander.
Heat oil/ghee to smoking point. Drop in mustard, cumin, and dried red chilies for a smoky, sharp tadka.
Turn off the heat, pour tadka over the curd rice just prior to serving.
Provides maximum crunch inside a cool bath.

recipeOptions.name: Roti with Bhindi (Okra)
instructions: Knead dough, make 2-3 thin, soft rotis/phulkas.
Wash and dry okra completely before chopping to prevent slime.
Heat oil, sauté sliced onions until pink.
Add okra, a pinch of turmeric, and cook uncovered on medium flame till okra softens.
Add coriander powder, salt, and amchur right at the end (adding salt early draws moisture). Toss and serve.

recipeOptions.name: Roti with Lauki (Bottle Gourd)
instructions: Make soft rotis.
Heat a small pressure cooker. Add oil, temper cumin seeds.
Saute chopped tomato until mushy. Add simple spices – turmeric, coriander, a pinch of chili.
Toss in diced lauki, add very little water (lauki has high moisture).
Pressure cook for 2 whistles. Garnish with coriander. Very light for digestion.

recipeOptions.name: Roti with Tinda (Apple Gourd)
instructions: Make basic phulkas.
In a pan/cooker, temper with cumin seeds. Gently sauté finely chopped onion and tomatoes.
Add chopped tinda, turmeric, chili powder, and salt.
Cook uncovered if pan, or 1 whistle if cooker, till tender but not completely dissolved.
An unassuming, incredibly light and cooling vegetable dish perfect for evening.

recipeOptions.name: Masala Veg Dalia
instructions: Dry roast Dalia on low flame till golden brown and aromatic. Wash Moong dal.
In a pressure cooker, heat heavily scented ghee, add cumin, ginger, onions, and sauté.
Toss in tomatoes, turmeric, mixed vegetables, dalia, and dal.
Add roughly 3.5 cups of water and salt.
Pressure cook for 3-4 whistles. Best enjoyed with a spoon of yogurt.

recipeOptions.name: Sweet Milk Dalia
instructions: Roast dalia in a little ghee till perfectly nutty.
Add a little water and pressure cook for 1-2 whistles just to soften it dramatically.
Open the cooker, pour in the warm milk, and simmer the soft dalia in the milk until thick (kheer-like).
Sweeten with sugar/jaggery, flavor with cardamom, and toss in sliced almonds.
Serve warm or chilled as a very soothing dinner dessert meal.

recipeOptions.name: Simple Namkeen Dalia (Pulao style)
instructions: Dry roast the dalia. Keep it aside.
In a wide pan, temper mustard, cumin, green chilies. Sauté peas and carrots briefly.
Add 2.5 cups of boiling water and salt. Tip the roasted dalia in.
Cover with a lid and cook purely on slow heat until all water is elegantly absorbed and grains separate.
Fluff up, squirt lemon juice, and serve dry like an upma/pulao.

recipeOptions.name: Plain Rice Dosa
instructions: Prepare hot, thin, crisp dosas without heavy masala filling.
For Chutney: Grind coconut, roasted chana dal, green chilies, tamarind, salt, and water.
Quick temper the chutney with mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil.
Serve 2-3 crispy plain dosas folded immediately off the tawa.
A delightfully light alternative to heavy rice.

recipeOptions.name: Neer Dosa
instructions: Soak raw rice for 4 hours. Grind to an extremely fine, smooth paste with grated coconut.
Dilute the batter substantially with water to make a milky, watery consistency.
Heat a cast-iron pan. Splash/pour the watery batter over it rapidly - do not spread with a ladle.
Cover and cook for 1-2 minutes. Do NOT flip the dosa. Fold into quarters and serve.
Pairs well with any light coconut-based gravy or jaggery-coconut mix.

recipeOptions.name: Wheat (Godhumai) Dosa
instructions: Whisk wheat flour, rice flour, cumin, lightly crushed pepper, chopped onions, salt, and lots of water.
Rest for 10 minutes. The batter should flow like thin buttermilk.
Pour the batter starting from the outer edges to the center of a smoking hot tawa.
Drizzle oil. Cook on medium flame until edges detach and crisp. Flip if needed.
Instant, zero-fermentation dosa.

recipeOptions.name: Idiyappam with Sweet Coconut Milk
instructions: Boil water with a pinch of salt and oil. Swiftly stir the boiled water into the rice flour.
Knead using a spoon (then hands when warm) into a smooth dough.
Press the dough through a sev press (idiyappam maker) onto greased steamer plates.
Steam for 5-7 minutes.
Sweeten thick coconut milk with jaggery and cardamom. Dunk warm idiyappams entirely into it.

recipeOptions.name: Idiyappam with Veg Stew
instructions: Similarly prepare, press, and steam the idiyappam.
Prepare a light, white, coconut-milk based stew as done for appams: gently boil veggies with spices, finish with thick coconut milk.
The savory, intensely spiced, but very light stew acts as a perfect blanket over fragile rice strings.

recipeOptions.name: Lemon Sevai (Lemon Idiyappam)
instructions: Make basic idiyappam, lightly steam them, and once cooled, pull the strands apart gently.
Heat oil, comprehensively roast peanuts, fenugreek, dry red chilies, mustard, curry leaves, and a large pinch of turmeric.
Take off heat. Pour lemon juice into the tempering.
Toss the separated rice strings into the bright yellow tadka.
A tangy, crunchy, ultra-light and quick dinner.

recipeOptions.name: Paneer Stuffed Moong Dal Chilla
instructions: Soak moong dal. Blend it with green chilies, ginger, salt, and minimal water to a medium-thick batter.
Mix grated paneer with chopped coriander and mild species.
Spread batter firmly on a hot tawa in concentric circles.
Cook till the bottom crisps, add handfuls of the paneer stuffing across the center.
Fold tightly like a crepe and slice. Serve hot.

recipeOptions.name: Plain Crispy Moong Chilla
instructions: For a higher protein variant, blend sprouted moong beans into a batter.
Spread the chilla wide on the tawa.
Sprinkle heavily the raw diced onions, tomatoes, and chaat masala directly onto the wet batter facing up.
Gently press the veggies down with a spatula. Flip quickly to slightly toast the vegetables.
Serve with mint chutney. No heavy stuffing required.

recipeOptions.name: Oats & Moong Mixed Chilla
instructions: Soak dal. Dry blend oats into flour. Optionally, blanche spinach briefly.
In a blender, thoroughly combine all ingredients: dal, oat flour, spinach, garlic, ginger, and yogurt to make a vibrant green batter.
Pour on hot tawa and spread it out medium-thick (oats make it slightly gummy if spread too thin).
Cook evenly on both sides.
A fiber and protein rich option that skips heavy grains completely.

recipeOptions.name: Kerala Chicken Roast (Nadan Kozhi Roast)

ingredients.name: Ginger Garlic, Green Chilies, Curry Leaves

instructions: Marinate chicken lightly in a pinch of chili and turmeric. Optionally, shallow fry it lightly first.
In intense heat with coconut oil, aggressively caramelize sliced onions, ginger-garlic paste, and loads of curry leaves.
Add spice powders, wait for oil to release, add tomatoes and cook to mush.
Add marinated/fried chicken, lower the heat, and mix the heavy thick onion masala all over it.
Roast continuously until it is dark, sticky, and completely dry. Serve with Appam or Parotta.

recipeOptions.name: Punjabi Dry Chicken (Chicken Sukha)
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and meat masala. Leave for 1 hour.
Splutter whole spices in oil, sauté roughly chopped onions until lightly pink.
Tip in the chicken and its marinade. Cook entirely on medium heat without adding raw water.
The chicken will release water. Stir and cook until the liquid entirely dries up and forms a concentrated dry masala coating.
Finish with lemon juice and a scattering of fresh coriander.

recipeOptions.name: Chicken Tikka Dry
instructions: Whisk hung curd, smoked mustard oil, kashmiri chili powder, ginger-garlic paste into a thick vivid red marinade.
Toss chicken chunks and veggies into it. Marinate for hours.
Skewer and grill them in an oven, or sear aggressively in a dry hot pan or air-fryer until nicely charred.
No gravy, no mess. Serve sizzling with green mint chutney and raw onion rings.

recipeOptions.name: Tandoori Paneer Tikka (Red)
instructions: Mix hung curd, aggressively hot mustard oil, kashmiri chili powder, roasted besan (binds the marinade), and tikka masala.
Gently coat thick paneer cubes, onion petals, and capsicum squares.
Grill on skewers inside a hot oven (200°C) or on a tawa until charred on edges.
Serve with lemon wedges and mint-coriander yogurt chutney.

recipeOptions.name: Hariyali (Green) Paneer Tikka
instructions: Blend thick, vibrant green mint and coriander leaves, along with garlic, ginger, and green chilies into a paste.
Whip the green paste directly into the hung curd with chaat masala and salt.
Coat paneer generously. Ensure the marinade isn't watery.
Pan-fry/grill until beautifully charred. The mint gives a vastly different, fresh profile compared to the heavy red tikka.

recipeOptions.name: Malai (Cream) Paneer Tikka
instructions: Prepare a luxurious, completely white marinade by whisking heavy cream, cream cheese, cashew paste, cardamom, and subtle white pepper.
Fold paneer cubes softly through the rich marinade. Cover intimately in film, rest.
Grill or bake exceptionally carefully, as cream burns quickly. Baste with butter.
The result is intensely creamy, mild, and melts in the mouth - brilliant for young kids.
